axis: stdlib / hexa-lang interp dispatcher residual sweep
title: residual dead-dispatch audit beyond fn call_builtin — 2 sites swept (1 in call_method, 1 in call_builtin)
summary: Generalize ω-stdlib-15's first-match-wins audit to the *other* large functions in self/hexa_full.hexa. Built a brace-depth + sibling-scope-aware awk pass over every `^fn NAME` block, scanning for repeated `<lhs> == "X"` and `<lhs> == TOK_X / TAG_X / NK_X` patterns within the same lexical scope path. Audited all 13 functions ≥ 200 LOC (call_builtin already swept by stdlib-15; call_method 1211, eval_expr_inner 935, exec_stmt_at 864, tokenize 622, call_user_fn 426, interpret 395, parse_primary 359, parse_stmt 292, ast_to_string 278, eval_binop 229, call_fn_val 218). Mostly negative result: scope-paired duplicates were nearly all *legitimately distinct conditions* (extra `tok.value == "_"`, extra `obj.tag == TAG_ARRAY`, extra `args[0].tag` discriminant, etc.) — false positives in raw lhs-string equality. TWO real dead-dispatch sites confirmed and removed: (a) call_method TAG_ERROR scope L15759 — `to_lowercase`/`to_uppercase` repeat, already covered by L15744's union return val_str(""); (b) call_builtin L14508 — the `name == "count"` disjunct in `if name == "str_count" || (name == "count" && ...)` is unreachable because the canonical `if name == "count"` at L14135 returns unconditionally (val_int(c) on TAG_ARRAY else val_int(0)). Replaced with `if name == "str_count"` only. Net: -3 LOC removed, +13 LOC of marker-comment context. This bounds stdlib-15's sweep — the dead-dispatcher pattern has now been audited across the full file, not just call_builtin.

predecessors.omega_stdlib_15: 247 LOC of dead first-match dispatchers swept from fn call_builtin

context.audit_method: awk fn-tracker + brace-depth + sibling-scope-id pass over self/hexa_full.hexa (18507 LOC). For each fn boundary (^fn NAME), maintain a stack scope_at_depth[] assigning a fresh scope_id every time depth increases (so sibling { } blocks at same depth get distinct ids). Key duplicates by full /scope-id/ path concat with the matched fragment. Patterns checked: (`name`|`method`|`op`|`tok`|`t`|`kind`|`cmd`|`key`|`tag`|`fname`|`sym`|`id`|`kid`|`nk`) `==` ("..." | TAG_X | NK_X | TOK_X). Also did a bare `else if X == "Y"` chain scan (no hits) and a `match X` head-scan (no dispatcher matches outside call_builtin's already-swept body).
false_positives_filtered: parse_stmt L1480 vs L1494 — `attr_name == "symbol"`: L1480 is union with `|| attr_name == "link"`, L1494 distinguishes inside the shared body — different conditions

parse_match_pattern L2728 vs L2733 — `tok.kind == "Ident"`: L2728 also requires `tok.value == "_"` (wildcard), L2733 also requires `p_peek_ahead(1).kind == "ColonColon"` (enum path) — different conditions

eval_expr_inner L7290 vs L7311 — `callee.left.kind == "Ident"`: L7290 routes to eval_effect_call, L7311 is gated by additional `obj.tag == TAG_ARRAY` and method `push|pop` for in-place mutation — different conditions
eval_expr_inner L7351 vs L7369 — `left_f.kind == "Ident"`: L7351 is the lookup branch, L7369 is gated by `obj.tag == TAG_VOID` for enum-variant resurrection — different conditions
exec_stmt_at, eval_binop, match_pattern, eval_call, sort_val_compare etc. — multiple `args[N].tag` checks in the same dispatcher are sister type-disambiguators, not dead duplicates
context.problem: After stdlib-15 swept fn call_builtin, residual dead-dispatch sites might still lurk in the parser/lexer/transpiler/evaluator helpers. Without an audit, future drift (someone editing the canonical site without realising a stale duplicate exists) remains a latent bug class.

edits.reason: L14135 `if name == "count"` is an unconditional dispatcher (returns val_int(c) on TAG_ARRAY else val_int(0)) — flow never reaches L14508 with name == "count". The disjunct was first-match-blocked dead.
edits.comment_added: 6-line marker referencing canonical L14135 + stdlib-15 lineage

edits.reason: First branch of TAG_ERROR scope (L15744) already returns val_str("") for `to_lowercase`/`to_uppercase` as part of `trim || to_lower || to_upper || to_lowercase || to_uppercase` union. Subsequent identical dispatch at L15759 was unreachable.
edits.comment_added: 5-line marker referencing the canonical first-branch + stdlib-15 lineage

functions_audited_top_13_by_loc.name: call_builtin
functions_audited_top_13_by_loc.note: stdlib-15 swept the bulk; this 1 was a partial-disjunct dead path it missed

audit_inventory.verdict: Residual dead-dispatch surface is empirically near-zero outside call_builtin. The stdlib-15 sweep was substantively complete; this cycle adds 2 marginal sites that the call_builtin-only audit missed (one was a partial-disjunct, not a full duplicate; one was in the sister fn call_method).

validation.fixture: exercises str_count(haystack, needle), count(arr, predicate), len(""), "abc".to_upper()
validation.verdict: all 4 surviving paths return expected values; renamed str_count works, canonical count reducer works, string method dispatch unaffected

validation.result: L14135 (canonical) only — dead L14508 disjunct removed
